name: A Coruña Coast Route
name_original: Coruña - vuelta por Torre de Hércules y Parque de Bens
description: An easy 15.7-mile mountain bike route in A Coruña, featuring the Tower of Hercules, coastal views, and Parque de Bens.
region_intro: Mountain biking around A Coruña offers diverse terrain, from its rugged Atlantic coastline to lush inland forests and hilly landscapes. The region is characterized by a "broken profile" with multiple climbs and descents, providing both physical challenge and scenic beauty. Impressive cliffs and numerous peaks, though under 200m are considered hills, contribute to varied singletracks and rewarding vistas. This varied terrain makes A Coruña a compelling destination for mountain biking enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around A Coruña The most popular mountain bike trail is Coruña - vuelta por Torre de Hércules y Parque de Bens, an easy 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route explores the iconic Tower of Hercules area and Parque de Bens, offering coastal views and varied paths. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Embalse de Cecebre – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Elviña-Universidade, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.4 km) path. This route winds through inland areas, offering views of the Cecebre Reservoir and surrounding natural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Tower of Hercules – View of the Tower of Hercules loop from Pescaría, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through coastal paths and urban green spaces, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Mountain biking around A Coruña is defined by its rugged Galician coastline, lush forests, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to technical challenges. The routes in A Coruña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore A Coruña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña offers a wide variety of mountain biking experiences, with over 60 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy rides to more challenging trails, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in A Coruña?
A: Yes, A Coruña has several easy mountain bike trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980541271/coruna-vuelta-por-torre-de-hercules-y-parque-de-bens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coruña - vuelta por Torre de Hércules y Parque de Bens&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 15.7-mile (25.3 km) trail that explores coastal views and varied paths around the iconic Tower of Hercules area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña's mountain biking terrain is incredibly diverse. You'll find rugged Atlantic coastline trails with magnificent ocean views and a 'broken profile' of climbs and descents. Inland, there are lush forests and hilly areas, ideal for technical riding, as well as well-maintained forest tracks, especially along sections of the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in A Coruña?
A: While specific family-designated routes aren't highlighted, the region's easier trails, such as those around the Tower of Hercules area, offer paths perfect for leisurely rides. These areas often feature flat sections and scenic views suitable for a family outing.

Q: Which mountain bike trails offer the best scenic views in A Coruña?
A: Many coastal routes provide spectacular views of the Atlantic. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1860479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Hercules&lt;/a&gt; offers paths with small coves and cliffs. For dramatic vistas, consider routes that pass by impressive cliffs like Vixía de Herbeira, one of Europe's highest above sea level, or trails leading to peaks like Moa Summit (Monte Pindo).

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in A Coruña?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in A Coruña are designed as loops. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Cecebre – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Elviña-Universidade&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 30.1-mile (48.4 km) path winding through natural landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38200309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tower of Hercules – View of the Tower of Hercules loop from Pescaría&lt;/a&gt;, which covers coastal paths and urban green spaces.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: A Coruña's trails often pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the area around the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1860479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Hercules&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón&lt;/a&gt;, a historic fortress, and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3093759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Carlos Garden&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls in A Coruña?
A: Yes, for those seeking trails with natural water features, the Refuge of the Greens in Coristanco is a notable area. It offers a mysterious natural space with wooden bridges, mills, and waterfalls, with routes extending into the surrounding forest.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña's climate allows for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal routes, though it can be busier. Winter rides are possible, but checking local trail conditions is always advisable due to potential rain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in A Coruña?
A: The mountain bike trails in A Coruña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging coastal climbs to scenic forest descents, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there options for advanced or technical mountain biking in A Coruña?
A: Absolutely. A Coruña caters to advanced riders with technical enduro and downhill challenges. Areas like the circuit of O Valiño in Oleiros are known for short, fun circuits with jumps and curves. The region's hilly inland terrain and dense woodlands also offer exciting descents and singletracks with features like berms, drops, and rock gardens.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in A Coruña?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on length and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teatro Colón – View of the Tower of Hercules loop from A Coruña - Provisional&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15 miles (24 km), can take around 1 hour 25 minutes. Longer, moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bens Park Dolmen – Sabón Beach loop from A Coruña - Provisional&lt;/a&gt; (24.7 miles / 39.8 km), might take over 2 hours 45 minutes.
tips: View of the Tower of Hercules. A very good bike lane on the coastal path. The oldest active lighthouse in Spain. built in the time of the roman empire. Today the tower is part of a recreational park that includes bike paths, a sculpture park and spectacular views of the sea.

A compacted gravel cycleway connecting O Portiño and the Bens beach. Great views of the atlantic and Illa redonda, specially during the afternoon and the sunset. The path goes around the water purification plant in Bens.
60-hectare green space converted from the old city garbage dump, with 13 km of recreational trails. Spectacular view of La Coruña and the sea

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is categorized as an easy mountain bike ride. It's suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels, offering a relaxed experience through varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of surfaces, including asphalt (12.2 km), paved sections (7.23 km), unpaved paths (3.07 km), gravel (1.48 km), and singletrack (3.33 km). A significant portion (11.5 km) follows dedicated cycleways, and about 10.48 km is on roads or paths. While mostly manageable, be prepared for some sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the historic View of the Tower of Hercules, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The route also takes you along the scenic Bens Coastal Cycle Path, offering great ocean views, and through Parque de Bens, where you can find the Bens Park Dolmen.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 25.3 km long and typically takes around 1 hour and 45 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: A Coruña's coastal climate makes this route enjoyable year-round. However, for the most pleasant experience with good visibility and comfortable temperatures, spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) are ideal. The Bens Coastal Cycle Path offers particularly stunning views at sunset.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Given its urban integration, you can find various parking options around the starting points near the Tower of Hercules or Parque de Bens. Look for public parking areas in A Coruña that offer convenient access to the coastal path sections.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, near wildlife, and within the designated park areas like Parque de Bens and around the Tower of Hercules, to ensure safety and respect for other users and the environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain bike route. Access to the Tower of Hercules park area and Parque de Bens is free for recreational use.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Paseo Marítimo, the Carril bici Metropolitano, and the Costa de Santo Amaro, among others, integrating into A Coruña's network of trails and cycleways.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this easy mountain bike route, bring your mountain bike, a helmet, water, and snacks. Depending on the weather, layers are advisable. While the trail is not overly technical, gloves and appropriate footwear are always recommended for comfort and safety.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its 'easy' difficulty rating and varied, mostly manageable terrain, this route is suitable for families with children who are comfortable riding mountain bikes. The dedicated cycleways and park sections offer a safe and enjoyable experience.

waypoints.name: View of the Tower of Hercules
